does: This paper systematically analyzes large language models as automated peer review assistants, surveying methods for two key tasks: generating written critiques and predicting reviewer scores. It presents a structured taxonomy of modeling approaches spanning prompt-based, supervised, retrieval-augmented, and alignment-optimized techniques, synthesizing empirical findings across existing benchmarks and datasets. The work evaluates current capabilities while identifying critical limitations through detailed analysis of dataset constraints, evaluation shortcomings, and domain concentration biases that undermine reliability assessments in contemporary peer review systems.
connects: This work builds on the growing literature exploring LLM applications in academic infrastructure, extending prior research on automated scientific evaluation that has examined whether neural models can approximate human reviewer judgments. It responds to recent studies showing LLMs can generate coherent critiques and score predictions, but acknowledges unresolved questions about their robustness and trustworthiness. The paper integrates perspectives from multiple research threads including machine learning evaluation methodology, adversarial robustness in NLP systems, and human-AI collaboration frameworks, connecting concerns about LLM reliability documented across domains.
enables: This survey establishes foundations for developing more trustworthy LLM-based review systems by documenting specific robustness vulnerabilities—prompt injection, data poisoning, retrieval failures, and reward hacking—that future work must address. It points toward research on adversarial robustness in peer review contexts, improved evaluation protocols that test domain generalization and genuine understanding rather than surface-level metrics, and secure human-AI collaboration frameworks. The systematic taxonomy and identified limitations create pathways for building verifiable, domain-aware systems that could eventually complement rather than replace human review while maintaining scientific integrity.
